headline: 3 Dividend Aristocrat ETFs to Buy Before 2026 Markets Shift
summary: Dividend growth investing rewards patience like few other strategies. The income stream compounds year after year, and the companies behind those dividends tend to be high-quality and structurally durable. With the Fed Funds rate at 3.75% and the 10-year Treasury yielding around 4.30%, dividend-focused equity funds must now compete against risk-free alternatives that offer meaningful ... 3 Dividend Aristocrat ETFs to Buy Before 2026 Markets Shift

headline: Coca-Cola vs. Johnson & Johnson: The Safest Dividend on Wall Street Might Surprise You
summary: Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) closed 2025 with steady organic growth and a 63rd consecutive dividend increase, while Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) opened 2026 with accelerating pharmaceutical momentum and its own 64-year dividend streak intact. Both are Dividend Kings built for uncertainty. But the businesses behind those credentials differ fundamentally, and that difference matters more than yield alone. ... Coca-Cola vs. Johnson & Johnson: The Safest Dividend on Wall Street Might Surprise You

headline: Did J&J's (JNJ) 64-Year Dividend Streak and Q1 Results Just Redefine Its Investment Narrative?
summary: Earlier this week, Johnson & Johnson reported first‑quarter 2026 results showing sales of US$24,062 million and net income of US$5,235 million, and its board approved a 3.1% increase in the quarterly dividend to US$1.34 per share, marking 64 consecutive years of dividend growth. At the same time, management highlighted momentum in newer medicines and MedTech innovations, reinforcing the company’s shift toward higher-growth therapies as older products face biosimilar competition. With Johnson...
